SOURCE_LANGUAGE: TypeScript
DOC FORMAT: TSDoc (compatible with TypeDoc, API Extractor, JSDoc renderers)

SYNTAX
======
- Block starts with /** on its own line, every continuation line with
  ' * ', closes with ' */'.
- Indentation matches the function/class/method declaration exactly.
- First line: concise, active one-liner. NO 'This function ...'.
- Blank line before the block tags.

BLOCK TAGS (in this order, only when applicable)
================================================
@param      <n> - <description ending with a period.>
@returns    <description ending with a period.>
@throws     <reason.>
@typeParam  <T> - <description of the generic parameter.>
@remarks    <multi-sentence elaboration if needed>
@example    <indented code example block>
@see        <reference>
@deprecated <reason + migration hint.>

CRITICAL TSDoc RULE
===================
NO type annotations in @param or @returns!
The types come from the TypeScript signature. Only name + description.

  WRONG:   @param {string} name - the name
  RIGHT:   @param name - the name

INLINE TAGS
===========
- {@link FullyQualified.Name}        - clickable symbol reference
- {@link FullyQualified.Name | text} - reference with anchor text
- {@inheritDoc OtherSymbol}          - inherit doc from a base symbol
- `code`                             - Markdown backticks for identifiers

RULES
=====
- Separate the @param name from the description with a hyphen:
  `name - description`.
- For destructured object parameters, document the properties individually:
    @param options - Configuration.
    @param options.timeout - Timeout in milliseconds.
- @returns: no type, only a description.
- @throws: do not put the error class as a type tag - use Markdown code
  in the description instead: `@throws \`RangeError\` when ...`
- For async functions/methods: mention what the Promise resolves with in
  the first line.
- Document type aliases and interfaces the same way as classes.
- @typeParam for generics: `@typeParam T - Element type of the list.`

EXAMPLE
=======
/**
 * Calculates the gross total including value-added tax.
 *
 * @param items - Line items to total.
 * @param taxRate - Tax rate as a decimal (e.g. `0.19` for 19 %).
 * @returns Gross total as a `number`.
 * @throws `RangeError` when `items` is empty.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * const total = calculateTotal([{ price: 10, quantity: 2 }], 0.19);
 * ```
 */
